title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for abnf, tokens, whitespace, line endings, limits, and parser architecture and requires the smallest strict strict bounded incremental parser architecture with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for strict bounded incremental parser architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Parse Validate Effect Phases
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 3651 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.
